steps.title: Fresh ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA)-treated peripheral blood...
steps.instruction: Fresh ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA)-treated peripheral blood from human individuals pre-screened for pathogenic viruses and HLA-DRB1-genotyped

steps.instruction: Heparin sodium 5000 i.u./mL solution (Leo Pharma, registration number: BR-

steps.instruction: Myelin peptides MBP83–99, MOG1–20, mMOG35–55, and hMOG35–55 (synthesized as previously described [20]).

steps.instruction: EDTA 0.5 M (pH 8) *Note: Initial water volume should be 200–300 mL as EDTA will take a substantial volume when fully dissolved. steps.instruction: Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS) *Note: Initial water volume should be 400 mL as salts will take a substantial volume when fully dissolved. Allow the solution to mix completely before adding any more water. pH should be adjusted to 7.4 using drops of 4 M NaOH. steps.instruction: 4% paraformaldehyde (PFA) *Note: Initial PBS volume should be 380 mL as PFA will take a substantial volume when fully dissolved. Allow the solution to mix completely before adding any more PBS. pH should be adjusted to 7.

steps.instruction: RPMI supplemented *Note: Addition of NaOH solution is for pH adjustment. The solution must be added 500 μL each time, as the final volume may vary. steps.title: ACK solution *Note
steps.instruction: ACK solution *Note: pH should be adjusted to 7.

steps.title: Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume NH 4 Cl 0
steps.instruction: Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume NH 4 Cl 0.15 M 4.3 g KHCO 3 10 mM 0.5 g EDTA 0.1 mM 100 μL of 0.5 EDTA solution H 2 O n/a 500 mL Total n/a 500 mL Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Autoclavable corn-cob bedding for IVC cages (Scobis-Due, Mucendola Sri, Italy)
steps.instruction: Autoclavable corn-cob bedding for IVC cages (Scobis-Due, Mucendola Sri, Italy)

steps.title: Mice are maintained in individually ventilated...
steps.instruction: Mice are maintained in individually ventilated cage (IVC) systems to safeguard and maintain their specific pathogen-free (SPF) microbiological status from the commercial supplier. Standard pelleted feed and chlorinated water are provided ad libitum to animals. Animals are housed under a 12/12 h light/dark photoperiod, 22–25 °C temperature, and 35%–75% humidity.

steps.title: Allow mice to acclimatize to the...
steps.instruction: Allow mice to acclimatize to the local facility’s conditions for a minimum of seven days. Ensure sterile certified enrichment material (sterile cotton swabs, mouse houses or igloo nests, paper tubes, etc.) in each cage and monitor the normal vital signs and behavior of the mice.

steps.title: Start the human-mouse habituation process, based...
steps.instruction: Start the human-mouse habituation process, based on a previous report [21,22], by gently cupping each mouse and allowing it to explore freely the experimenter’s palm for 1–3 min daily for 3–5 consecutive days. Since the B2m-NOG strain is extremely immunodeficient, all procedures should be performed inside the BSL-2 laminar flow cabinet for animals. Contact time and duration depend on the behavior of each animal, whose respiratory rate, urination, biting, or escaping efforts are noted and assessed as welfare indicators. The same animal should be handled by the same animal user.

steps.title: Proceed to mouse training for tail...
steps.instruction: Proceed to mouse training for tail blood sampling by gently introducing them to the mouse restrainer, petting and positively reinforcing them by offering them a treat (irradiated sunflower seeds, fruit gels, etc.). This step lasts 3–5 min/mouse/day for 3 days. Afterward, add the tail puncturing step and always monitor the welfare indicators. A total of 6–8 days is required for tail bleeding training.

steps.title: Trained animals are used for the animal study
steps.instruction: Trained animals are used for the animal study. At the end of each handling and procedure (injections, bleeding, symptom scoring, etc.), mice are offered a treat. For the EAE model, refinement is applied (i.e., soft bedding, water-gels with glucose), and clinical endpoints are monitored daily. B. PBMC isolation from human peripheral blood For the study using the humanization model described here, PBMCs were isolated from the peripheral blood of six long-term RRMS patients, of which five were HLA-DRB1*15-positive (DR15 MS1–

steps.title: and one was HLA-DRB1*13-positive (DR13 MS),...
steps.instruction: and one was HLA-DRB1*13-positive (DR13 MS), all presenting with highly active disease following immunomodulatory treatment with natalizumab. A DR15-positive healthy individual (DR15 HI) was selected as a control donor [1]. CAUTION: Human blood donors should be pre-screened for pathogenic viruses, and fresh blood should be handled in a BSL-2 biosafety cabinet.

steps.title: One hour before the blood draw,...
steps.instruction: One hour before the blood draw, fill a 50 mL tube with 0.5 M EDTA solution (pH

steps.title: to perform coating
steps.instruction: to perform coating. All procedures of this stage must be performed inside the high-level BSL-2 biosafety cabinet.

steps.title: Remove the EDTA solution and leave...
steps.instruction: Remove the EDTA solution and leave about 100 μL for each 10 mL of blood. For 50 mL of blood, the tube must contain 500 μL of EDTA solution. Note: Blood could alternatively be collected in 10 mL EDTA Vacutainers.

steps.title: Transfer the fresh EDTA-treated blood at...
steps.instruction: Transfer the fresh EDTA-treated blood at RT on the same day to the laboratory.

steps.title: Keep 5 mL of the whole...
steps.instruction: Keep 5 mL of the whole blood aside for flow cytometry immunoprofiling and plasma isolation.

steps.title: Aliquot the remaining 45 mL of...
steps.instruction: Aliquot the remaining 45 mL of blood into three different 50 mL tubes (15 mL each) and dilute each with an equal volume of PBS. Note: Optimization of the PBMC preparation protocol: Fresh human PBMCs were used for the generation of humanized immune mice using this transplantation protocol [1]. Alternatives include blood diluted 1:1 with RPMI and stored at 4 °C for 72 h prior to PBMC preparation, or fresh PBMCs snap-frozen, stored at -80 °C, thawed, and cultured for 24 h, although these alternative procedures were found to be sub-optimal and showed skewing of immune cell lineage subsets. See Figure 1 , Figure supplement 2 of the original research paper [1].

steps.title: Add 15 mL of Ficoll-Histopaque ®...
steps.instruction: Add 15 mL of Ficoll-Histopaque ® -1077 to three separate clean 50 mL tubes.

steps.title: Carefully overlay the diluted fresh blood...
steps.instruction: Carefully overlay the diluted fresh blood over the Ficoll-Histopaque ® -

steps.title: CAUTION
steps.instruction: CAUTION: In this step, the overlay has to be done very carefully so that the Ficoll does not mix with the blood.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 800× g for 25...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 800× g for 25 min at 25 °C (RT), with acceleration: 1 and deceleration:

steps.title: Remove some of the upper “plasma”...
steps.instruction: Remove some of the upper “plasma” layer to gain easier access to the buffy coat.

steps.title: Collect the buffy coat and transfer...
steps.instruction: Collect the buffy coat and transfer it into separate clean 15 mL tubes (from each 50 mL tube, the buffy coat should be divided into two separate 15 mL tubes). The buffy coat is located at the interface of plasma and Ficoll ( Figure 1 ). CAUTION: Ensure the different phases do not mix.

steps.title: Add 10 mL of 2% FBS/PBS...
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of 2% FBS/PBS and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at RT, with acceleration: 7 and deceleration:

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 2 mL of Quicklysis erythrocyte cell lysis buffer, and incubate at RT for 10 min. Note: Erythrocyte lysis can alternatively be achieved using ACK solution at RT for 2 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge: 300× g , 10 min, RT, acceleration: 7, deceleration:

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of 2% FBS/PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge: 300× g , 10 min, RT, acceleration: 7, deceleration:

steps.title: Remove supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of HBSS, and put 2 μL in a 1.5 mL tube. Dilute cells in the tube with 198 μL of PBS and count using a Neubauer chamber.

steps.title: Adjust cell concentration to 50 ×...
steps.instruction: Adjust cell concentration to 50 × 10 6 cells/mL in HBSS solution. The cell suspension is ready to be injected into mice. Figure

steps.title: Peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) isolation protocol
steps.instruction: Peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) isolation protocol. Fresh whole peripheral blood is diluted with an equal volume of PBS, gently layered over Ficoll-Histopaque ® -

steps.title: The tube is placed in the centrifuge for gradient centrifugation
steps.instruction: The tube is placed in the centrifuge for gradient centrifugation. The PBMC buffy coat is located at the interface of Ficoll and plasma, as indicated (arrow). C. EBV reactivation status in plasma from PBMC donors Plasma is isolated from 5 mL of total fresh EDTA-treated peripheral blood using the following protocol:

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge: 1,200× g , 15 min, 23 °C, acceleration: 7, deceleration:

steps.title: Carefully collect plasma (supernatant) with a...
steps.instruction: Carefully collect plasma (supernatant) with a 1,000 μL pipette tip so that you do not disrupt the cell pellet.

steps.title: Transfer plasma to 1
steps.instruction: Transfer plasma to 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Store at -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store at -80 °C.

steps.title: When ready to perform the ELISA...
steps.instruction: When ready to perform the ELISA assay, thaw plasma at RT.

steps.title: In our study [1], donor plasma...
steps.instruction: In our study [1], donor plasma was screened for the following six EBV antibody markers: anti-viral capsid antigen (VCA) EBV IgG, anti-VCA EBV IgM, anti-VCA EBV IgA, anti-early antigen (D) [EA(D)] EBV IgG, anti-Epstein–Barr nuclear antigen 1 (EBNA-

steps.title: EBV IgG, and anti-EBNA-1 EBV IgM,...
steps.instruction: EBV IgG, and anti-EBNA-1 EBV IgM, although this is an optional step.

steps.title: Dilute plasma 1:100 in dilution buffer...
steps.instruction: Dilute plasma 1:100 in dilution buffer (provided by the VIDIA kits).

steps.title: Perform ELISA assays according to the manufacturer’s instructions (VIDIA)
steps.instruction: Perform ELISA assays according to the manufacturer’s instructions (VIDIA).

steps.title: Measure the intensity of the color...
steps.instruction: Measure the intensity of the color reaction using a spectrophotometer/colorimeter (Tecan Spark® or equivalent) at 450 nm within 20 min of stopping the reaction. A 620–690 nm reference filter is recommended.

steps.title: The interpretation of EBV reactivation status...
steps.instruction: The interpretation of EBV reactivation status is based on the criteria listed in Table 1 . Note: Each reaction’s volume is 100 μL per well, and it is advisable to use duplicates for each ELISA EBV test. Table

steps.title: Clinical interpretation of EBV reactivation status...
steps.instruction: Clinical interpretation of EBV reactivation status VCA EBV EA(D) EBV EBNA-1 EBV Stages of EBV infection IgG IgM IgA IgG IgM IgG* IgM* - - - - - - - Seronegative - + + - + or - - + Primoinfection (early phase) + low avidity + + + or - + or - - + Primoinfection + - + or - + or - - + - + + or - + or - - - + high avidity + - + or - - + - Suspect reactivation - + + or - - + - - - ++ - + - + high avidity - - - - + - Seropositivity without symptoms of active infection *Findings of abnormally high levels of both IgG and IgM anti-EBNA-1 antibody classes indicate EBV anomalous reactivation and a possible autoimmune condition. Epstein–Barr virus (EBV), viral capsid antigen (VCA), early antigen [EA(D)], Epstein–Barr nuclear antigen 1 (EBNA-1), immunoglobulin G (IgG), immunoglobulin M (IgM), immunoglobulin A (IgA). D. Mouse engraftment with PBMC

steps.title: In the animal house, place the...
steps.instruction: In the animal house, place the stand, mouse restrainer, and infrared thermal lamp into the BSL-2 laminar flow cabinet for animals.

steps.title: Turn on UV for 10 min. Note
steps.instruction: Turn on UV for 10 min. Note: You should leave the room during these 10 min to avoid UV radiation.

steps.title: Turn on the laminar flow and...
steps.instruction: Turn on the laminar flow and place the mouse cage inside the cabinet.

steps.title: Place a mouse in the mouse...
steps.instruction: Place a mouse in the mouse restrainer and secure it with the stand.

steps.title: Place the tail close (~10 cm...
steps.instruction: Place the tail close (~10 cm distance) to the infrared thermal lamp for 30 s.

steps.title: Using a cotton swab, gently sterilize...
steps.instruction: Using a cotton swab, gently sterilize the tail with 70% ethanol by wiping the skin toward the tail tip.

steps.title: Use a fiber light source to...
steps.instruction: Use a fiber light source to locate the lateral mouse tail vein. Draw 200 μL (10 × 10 6 cells) of resuspended cell suspension into a 1 mL insulin syringe with a 29G needle and inject slowly into the mouse tail vein (intravenous injection) ( Figure 2 ). CAUTION: This step needs a lot of stability so that the cell suspension is injected smoothly into the tail vein. Push the plunger in slowly and steadily until the required volume has been injected. If the needle is in the vein, there will be no resistance. If the needle is not in the vein, there will be resistance, and proceeding with the injection is not advised. Figure

steps.title: Peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) transplantation into B2m-NOG mice
steps.instruction: Peripheral blood mononuclear cell (PBMC) transplantation into B2m-NOG mice. A B2m-NOG mouse is placed in the restrainer, and the tail is placed close to the thermal lamp (~10 cm distance). The restrainer is secured by a stand, and the tail is sterilized with 70% ethanol using a cotton swab. Using a fiber optic light source for easier visualization of the veins, 10 × 10 6 PBMCs are injected intravenously into a lateral tail vein.

steps.title: Gently remove the needle while pressing...
steps.instruction: Gently remove the needle while pressing the injection site with a cotton swab to stop bleeding.

steps.title: Gently place the mouse back into the cage
steps.instruction: Gently place the mouse back into the cage. Injected mice should be weighed and monitored daily for their well-being and possible development of spontaneous clinical symptoms (see Section G). E. Human peripheral blood flow cytometry immunoprofiling

steps.title: Place 100 μL of whole blood into a 1
steps.instruction: Place 100 μL of whole blood into a 1.5 mL tube.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Quicklysis erythrocyte...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Quicklysis erythrocyte lysis buffer and incubate for 20 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge
steps.instruction: Centrifuge: 300× g , 10 min, RT, acceleration: 7, deceleration:

steps.title: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 2...
steps.instruction: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 2 μL of each of the following antibodies ( Table 2 ) in a clean 1.5 mL tube. Table

steps.title: Pan-leukocytes 2 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human...
steps.instruction: Pan-leukocytes 2 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD3 (clone HIT3a) T lymphocytes 1 μg/mL PE-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD4 (clone A161A

steps.title: T helper cells 2 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK
steps.instruction: T helper cells 2 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK

steps.title: T cytotoxic cells 1 μg/mL PE anti-human CD19 (clone 4G
steps.instruction: T cytotoxic cells 1 μg/mL PE anti-human CD19 (clone 4G

steps.title: B lymphocytes 4 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5
steps.instruction: B lymphocytes 4 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5.1H

steps.title: Natural killer cells 4 μg/mL APC anti-human CD66b (clone G1OF
steps.instruction: Natural killer cells 4 μg/mL APC anti-human CD66b (clone G1OF

steps.title: Remove supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μL of PBS, and add antibody mastermix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at RT
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at RT.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge: 300× g , 10 min, RT, acceleration: 7, deceleration:

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of PBS (300–350 μL), and transfer to flow cytometry tubes.

steps.title: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cell...
steps.instruction: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cell cytometer or similar [equipped with three lasers: blue, red, and violet (4B-2R-3V configuration)]. The cytometer must be able to detect the fluorochromes of the antibodies listed in Table 2 . F. Blood sampling and flow cytometry immunoprofiling of mouse peripheral blood

steps.title: Before going to the animal house,...
steps.instruction: Before going to the animal house, place 10 μL of heparin into 1.5 mL tubes (one for each mouse).

steps.title: In the animal house, place the...
steps.instruction: In the animal house, place the stand, mouse restrainer, infrared thermal lamp, pipette, tubes, and tips into the BSL-2 laminar flow cabinet for animals.

steps.title: Turn on UV for 10 min. Note
steps.instruction: Turn on UV for 10 min. Note: You should leave the room during these 10 min to avoid UV radiation.

steps.title: Turn on the laminar flow and...
steps.instruction: Turn on the laminar flow and place the mouse cage inside the cabinet.

steps.title: Place a trained mouse (see Section...
steps.instruction: Place a trained mouse (see Section D) in the mouse restrainer and secure it with the stand.

steps.title: Place the tail close to the...
steps.instruction: Place the tail close to the infrared thermal lamp (~10 cm distance).

steps.title: Using a cotton swab, gently sterilize...
steps.instruction: Using a cotton swab, gently sterilize the tail with 70% ethanol by wiping skin toward the tail tip.

steps.title: Use a fiber light source to...
steps.instruction: Use a fiber light source to locate the mouse’s tail vein. Using a fine needle, puncture the vein, starting close to the tip and progressively working toward the tail base in successive draws, and collect the blood using a pipette with a 200 μL tip.

steps.title: Repeat steps F7–8 until you collect...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps F7–8 until you collect at least 50–80 μL of blood.

steps.title: Gently press the tail with a...
steps.instruction: Gently press the tail with a cotton swab to stop bleeding and place the mouse back in the cage.

steps.title: In the laboratory, add 1 mL...
steps.instruction: In the laboratory, add 1 mL of Quicklysis erythrocyte lysis buffer to the tubes and incubate for 25 min at RT.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at RT
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at RT.

steps.title: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 1...
steps.instruction: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 1 μL of each of the following antibodies ( Table 3 ) in a new 1.5 mL tube containing 100 μL of PBS for each sample (e.g., if you have 8 samples, you have to put 800 μL of PBS in the tube. Making mastermix for 2–3 extra samples is recommended). Table

steps.title: Pan-leukocytes 1 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human...
steps.instruction: Pan-leukocytes 1 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD3 (clone HIT3a) T lymphocytes 0.5 μg/mL PE-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD4 (clone A161A

steps.title: T helper cells 1 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK
steps.instruction: T helper cells 1 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK

steps.title: T cytotoxic cells 0
steps.instruction: T cytotoxic cells 0.5 μg/mL PE anti-human CD19 (clone 4G

steps.title: B lymphocytes 2 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5
steps.instruction: B lymphocytes 2 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5.1H

steps.title: Natural killer cells 2 μg/mL APC anti-mouse CD45 (clone 30-F
steps.instruction: Natural killer cells 2 μg/mL APC anti-mouse CD45 (clone 30-F

steps.title: Pan-leukocytes (mouse) 2 μg/mL BV421 anti-human CD14 (clone HCD
steps.instruction: Pan-leukocytes (mouse) 2 μg/mL BV421 anti-human CD14 (clone HCD

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μL of the antibody mastermix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PBS and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Resuspend the cell pellet in an...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of PBS (300–350 μL) and transfer to a flow cytometry tube.

steps.title: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cell...
steps.instruction: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cell cytometer or similar cytometer [equipped with three lasers: blue, red, and violet (4B-2R-3V configuration)]. The cytometer must be able to detect the fluorochromes of the antibodies listed in Table 3 . This procedure should be performed weekly to monitor human immune cell engraftment. G. EAE immunization with myelin peptides

steps.title: Immunization is performed by subcutaneous (s
steps.instruction: Immunization is performed by subcutaneous (s.c.) injection of an emulsion containing myelin peptides, which is prepared fresh on the day of immunization using the following recipe ( Table 4 ). The total volume of emulsion for each injection is 200 μL. Table

steps.title: Myelin peptide cocktail for induction of...
steps.instruction: Myelin peptide cocktail for induction of EAE in PBMC humanized B2m-NOG mice Reagent Dose/mouse Quantity/mouse For 10 mice Total 200 μL 200 μL 2,000 μL** Mouse MOG35–55 100 μg (stock: 10 μg/μL) 10 μL 100 μL Human MOG35–55 100 μg (stock: 10 μg/μL) 10 μL 100 μL MOG1–20 100 μg (stock: 10 μg/μL) 10 μL 100 μL MBP83–99 100 μg (stock: 10 μg/μL) 20 μL 200 μL H37Ra Mycobacterium tuberculosis 400 μg 400 μg 4,000 μg Saline n/a * 50 μL 500 μL FCA 100 μL 100 μL 1,000 μL MOG: myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein; MBP: myelin basic protein; FCA: Freund’s complete adjuvant. *The saline volume is adjusted so that the final volume of the peptides + saline is 100 μL. CAUTION: **The emulsion is very viscous, and there is loss during the syringe loading and injection procedures. It is recommended to make one extra dose calculated for every 4 mice.

steps.title: Place the appropriate amount of desiccated...
steps.instruction: Place the appropriate amount of desiccated Mycobacterium tuberculosis in the glass Douche homogenizer and add saline stepwise in parts of 250 µL. CAUTION: The use of a protective mask is recommended.

steps.title: Use the pestle to dissolve the...
steps.instruction: Use the pestle to dissolve the Mycobacterium tuberculosis in the saline.

steps.title: Transfer the mixture using a glass...
steps.instruction: Transfer the mixture using a glass pipette into a 7 mL polystyrene container.

steps.title: Add the appropriate amount of myelin...
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate amount of myelin peptides and mix using a vortex shaker.

steps.title: Aliquot the mixture (maximum 700 μL) into clean 1
steps.instruction: Aliquot the mixture (maximum 700 μL) into clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tubes.

steps.title: Add an equal volume of FCA to each tube (e
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume of FCA to each tube (e.g., if the volume of the mixture is 500 μL, add 500 μL of FCA).

steps.title: Sonicate in intervals (15 s on,...
steps.instruction: Sonicate in intervals (15 s on, 10 s off, RT) until the mixture becomes an emulsion. Do a short sharp spin if needed (should not exceed 800× g). CAUTION: Avoid overheating the emulsion during sonication.

steps.title: Load emulsion on 1 mL BD Plastipak syringes
steps.instruction: Load emulsion on 1 mL BD Plastipak syringes. Hit the loaded syringes sideways on a hard, flat surface to remove bubbles toward the opening.

steps.title: Prepare Bordetella pertussis toxin at a...
steps.instruction: Prepare Bordetella pertussis toxin at a dose of 200 ng/injection by diluting 1 μL of toxin stock solution (concentration: 0.2 μg/μL stock) in 100 μL of saline for one injection.

steps.title: In the animal house, place all...
steps.instruction: In the animal house, place all materials in the laminar flow cabinet and turn on UV for 10 min. Note: You should leave the room during these 10 min to avoid UV radiation.

steps.title: Turn on the laminar flow and...
steps.instruction: Turn on the laminar flow and place the mouse cage inside the cabinet.

steps.title: Hold the mouse facing down, shave...
steps.instruction: Hold the mouse facing down, shave the hair on one hind flank close to the tail base, and inject 200 μL of emulsion subcutaneously (s.c.) at this site ( Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: Experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis (EAE) immunization with myelin peptides
steps.instruction: Experimental autoimmune encephalomyelitis (EAE) immunization with myelin peptides. Mice are injected subcutaneously on one hind flank close to the tail base with a Freund’s complete adjuvant (FCA) emulsion containing mMOG35–55, hMOG35–55, MOG1–20, and MBP83–99 (100 μg each peptide). Mice are also injected intraperitoneally with Bordetella pertussis (200 ng) on the day of immunization and 48 h later.

steps.title: Hold the mouse abdomen up and...
steps.instruction: Hold the mouse abdomen up and inject 100 μL of Bordetella pertussis toxin intraperitoneally (i.p.). This injection should be repeated 48 h later.

steps.title: Mice should be weighed and monitored...
steps.instruction: Mice should be weighed and monitored daily for their overall mobility and well-being and for the development of any spontaneous clinical symptoms typical of EAE (using scores described in Table 5 , with 0.5 gradations representing intermediate scores), neurological dysfunction, or graft-versus-host disease (GvHD) (see Papazian et al. [1]). Table

steps.title: Clinical scoring of EAE Score Clinical...
steps.instruction: Clinical scoring of EAE Score Clinical Symptoms 0 Normal 1 Limp tail 2 Hind limb weakness 3 Hind limb paralysis 4 Forelimb paralysis 5 Moribund or dead H. Mouse splenocyte isolation (summarized in Figure 4 )

steps.title: Remove the spleen and place it...
steps.instruction: Remove the spleen and place it on a 94 × 16 mm Petri dish containing 5 mL of RPMI. Transfer the Petri dishes into a BSL-2 laminar flow cabinet for mouse cells. All procedures should be performed inside the BSL-2 laminar flow cabinet until the cells are fixed.

steps.title: Dissociate the spleen using the rubber...
steps.instruction: Dissociate the spleen using the rubber seal end of a 5 mL syringe plunger.

steps.title: Transfer the splenocyte suspension into a 50 mL tube
steps.instruction: Transfer the splenocyte suspension into a 50 mL tube. Wash the Petri dish with an additional 5 mL of RPMI and add the wash to the cells.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of Gey’s Solution. Leave at RT for 5 min.

steps.title: Add 10 mL of RPMI and...
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL of RPMI and pass the cell suspension through a 70 μm cell strainer into a new 50 mL tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 5 mL of RPMI. For cell surface marker staining, transfer 1 mL of cell suspension (containing at least 10 × 10 6 cells) into a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube for fixation. For intracellular cytokine measurement by flow cytometry, transfer 2 mL of cell suspension (containing at least 20 × 10 6 cells) into a clean 15 mL tube and proceed to Section I.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1 mL of PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 400 μL of PBS. Slowly add 400 μL of 4% PFA under gentle agitation.

steps.title: Incubate at 4 °C for 20 min
steps.instruction: Incubate at 4 °C for 20 min.

steps.title: Add 600 μL of PBS and...
steps.instruction: Add 600 μL of PBS and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 400 μL of PBS. Store at 4 °C until further processing. I. Splenocyte preparation for intracellular cytokine measurement

steps.title: Perform steps H1–
steps.instruction: Perform steps H1–

steps.title: For intracellular cytokine measurement by flow...
steps.instruction: For intracellular cytokine measurement by flow cytometry, transfer 2 mL of cell suspension (containing at least 20 × 10 6 cells) into a clean 15 mL tube and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 1.5 mL of cytokine stimulation cocktail. Transfer into a clean 1.5 mL tube ( Table 6 ). Table

steps.title: T-cell stimulation cocktail for intracellular cytokine...
steps.instruction: T-cell stimulation cocktail for intracellular cytokine detection by flow cytometry Reagent Stock concentration Working concentration Dilution For 1 sample For 10 samples PMA 1 mg/mL 20 ng/mL 1/5,000 0.2 μL 2 μL Ionomycin 1 mg/mL 1 μg/mL 1/1,000 1 μL 10 μL Brefeldin A 5 mg/mL 5 μg/mL 1/1,000 1 μL 10 μL RPMI 1.5 mL 15 mL

steps.title: Incubate for 3 h at 37...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 3 h at 37 °C with 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Splenocyte isolation
steps.instruction: Splenocyte isolation. Spleens are placed on a Petri dish, dissociated, and transferred to a 50 mL tube and centrifuged. They are washed with PBS, centrifuged, and resuspended in Gey’s solution for red blood cell (RBC) lysis. They are centrifuged and resuspended in RPMI. One part is fixed for surface staining, and another part is stimulated for intracellular cytokine measurement and fixed afterward. J. Flow cytometry surface staining and acquisition

steps.title: For cell surface marker staining of...
steps.instruction: For cell surface marker staining of fixed splenocytes, wash cells with 1 mL of PBS.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 1...
steps.instruction: Prepare antibody mastermix by mixing 1 μL of each of the following antibodies ( Table 7 ) in a clean 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube containing 100 μL of PBS for each sample (e.g., for 8 samples, place 800 μL of PBS in the tube; making mastermix for 2–3 extra samples is recommended). Table

steps.title: Pan-leukocytes 1 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human...
steps.instruction: Pan-leukocytes 1 μg/mL APC-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD3 (clone HIT3a) T lymphocytes 0.5 μg/mL PE-Cyanine 7 anti-human CD4 (clone A161A

steps.title: T helper cells 1 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK
steps.instruction: T helper cells 1 μg/mL PerCP anti-human CD8 (clone SK

steps.title: T cytotoxic cells 0
steps.instruction: T cytotoxic cells 0.5 μg/mL PE anti-human CD19 (clone 4G

steps.title: B lymphocytes 2 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5
steps.instruction: B lymphocytes 2 μg/mL FITC anti-human CD56 (clone 5.1H

steps.title: Natural killer cells 2 μg/mL APC anti-mouse CD45 (clone 30-F
steps.instruction: Natural killer cells 2 μg/mL APC anti-mouse CD45 (clone 30-F

steps.title: Pan-leukocytes (mouse) 2 μg/mL BV421 anti-human CD14 (clone HCD
steps.instruction: Pan-leukocytes (mouse) 2 μg/mL BV421 anti-human CD14 (clone HCD

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μL of the antibody mastermix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PBS and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of PBS (300–350 μL), and transfer to flow cytometry tubes.

steps.title: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cytometer...
steps.instruction: Acquire data with a FACSMelody cytometer or similar [equipped with three lasers: blue, red, and violet (4B-2R-3V configuration)]. The cytometer must be able to detect the fluorochromes of the antibodies listed in Table 7 . K. Flow cytometry intracellular staining and acquisition

steps.title: For intracellular staining of cytokines in...
steps.instruction: For intracellular staining of cytokines in fixed splenocytes, aliquot cells into two different clean 1.5 mL tubes and add 1 mL of permeabilization buffer to the cells. Incubate at RT for 15 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Prepare two different antibody mastermixes by...
steps.instruction: Prepare two different antibody mastermixes by mixing the following antibodies in two clean 1.5 mL microfuge tubes and making up to 100 μL with PBS/sample (Tables 8 and 9). Making mastermix for 2–3 extra samples is recommended. Note: The two antibody panels (Tables 8 and

steps.title: can be collapsed into one by...
steps.instruction: can be collapsed into one by using another fluorochrome for either IFN-γ or IL-17A. Table

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in 100 μL of the appropriate flow cytometry antibody mastermix.

steps.title: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate for 30 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of PBS and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of PBS and centrifuge at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant, resuspend the cell pellet in an appropriate volume of PBS (300–350 μL), and transfer to flow cytometry tubes.

steps.title: Acquire data with a FACSCalibur cytometer...
steps.instruction: Acquire data with a FACSCalibur cytometer or similar (the cytometer must be equipped with two lasers, blue and red, and must be able to detect the fluorochromes of the antibodies listed in Tables 8 and 9).

description: Validation of protocol Human immune system engraftment In our original study by Papazian et al. [1], transplanted HIS mice were monitored for human immune cell engraftment by flow cytometry analysis of small blood samples recovered from the tail vein at different time points (7, 13, 42 dpt) and the spleen at sacrifice on 42 dpt; all showed progressive engraftment by human CD45+ (hCD45+) leukocytes from Day 7 ( Figure 1A from [1]; Figure 1 , Figure supplement 4A from [1]). Flow cytometry analysis showed preferential expansion of hCD4+ T lymphocytes compared to hCD8+ T lymphocytes in blood and spleens of all engrafted mice, and of hCD19+ B cells in mice engrafted with PBMC from several donors ( Figure 1B , C from [1]; Figure 1 , Figure supplement 4B from [1]). Notably, B-cell engraftment was best in MS patients interpreted as having suspected recent or ongoing reactivation of EBV, as determined by plasma levels of anti-EBV antibodies (Table 1 from [1]; Figure 1 , Figure supplement 1C from [1]). Human immune cells other than T and B cells, notably monocytes, were undetectable in dpt 42 spleen. Analysis of cytokine production by intracellular staining showed high proportions of interferon-γ (IFN-γ)-producing hCD4+ and hCD8+ splenocytes, and IL-17A-producing hCD4+ and hCD4− splenocytes in both immunized and non-immunized mice ( Figure 1D from [1]). Functionality of the human immune system in B2m-NOG mice was validated using an ex vivo proliferation assay (CFSE dilution assay) for T-cell responses in splenocytes with the four myelin peptides used for immunization (mMOG35–55, hMOG35–55, MOG1–20, and MBP83–99), and positive myelin-specific T-cell proliferation responses were measured in mice engrafted with PBMC from DR13 MS donors, although not DR15 MS donors. Neurological symptoms and signs of GVHD In our study [1], none of the typical clinical signs of EAE or other neurological deficits were observed in any of the HIS mice in the timeframe of the study (42 days dpt).
